incorrects.description: Student placed wrong number of symbols on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Count the Bird sightings in the table—how many symbols do you need?

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong number of symbols on second attempt

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Birds shows 7 sightings. Each full symbol stands for 2, so 3 full symbols is 6. You need one more sighting—that's a half-symbol.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong number of symbols three or more times — system models the answer

params.description: System places 3.5 symbols in Birds row

steps.text: This is tricky, so let me show you. Birds has 7 sightings. Each full symbol is 2, so I place 3 full symbols—that's 6 sightings. Then I need 1 more, so I place a half-symbol. 6 plus 1 equals 7.
